knowledge.html:
1. To infinitive (to V)

Động từ nguyên mẫu co to được dùng lam:

  • Chủ ngữ cua cau
    Ex: To become a teacher is my dream.
  • Bổ ngữ cho chủ ngữ
    Ex: What she likes is to swim in the sea and then to lie on the sand.
  • Tân ngữ cua động từ
    Ex: It was a boring holiday, so we decided to take a trip to Singapore.
  • Tân ngữ cua tính từ
    Ex: I'm very happy to go to school after Tet holiday.

Nhung động từ theo sau la "to V"

STTCấu trúcNghia
1Decide to VQuyet dinh làm gì
2Want to VMuốn làm gì
3Agree to VĐồng ý làm gì
4Expect to VMong muon làm gì
5Fail to VThat bai làm gì
6Hope to VHy vong làm gì
7Promise to VHứa làm gì
8Manage to VXoay so làm gì
9Prepare to VChuan bi làm gì
10Would like to VMuốn, thich làm gì
11Pretend to VGia vo làm gì
12Plan to VCo kế hoạch làm gì
13Ask to VYêu cầu làm gì
14Afford to VCo du khả năng làm gì
15Choose to VChon làm gì
16Intend to VDu dinh làm gì
17Refuse to VTừ chối làm gì
18Attempt to VNo luc làm gì
19Offer to VĐề nghị làm gì
20Vow to VThe làm gì

2. Bare infinitive (V nguyên thể)

  • Dùng sau cac động từ khuyet thieu: can, may, should,...
    Ex: I can speak English and France.
  • Dùng sau cac động từ: had better, would rather, had sooner.
    Ex: You had better study harder.
  • Dùng sau cac động từ: let/make.
    Ex: He made me cry.

3. Gerund (Ving): danh động từ

Danh động từ được dùng lam:

  • Chủ ngữ cua cau
    Ex: Skiing is my favorite sport.
  • Bổ ngữ cua động từ
    Ex: My hobby is collecting coins.
  • Tân ngữ cua động từ
    Ex: I like travelling.

Nhung động từ đi với V-ing

STTCấu trúcNghia
1Can't help/can't stand/can't bearKhong the chịu đựng duoc
2Enjoy/fancy VingThich làm gì
3Delay/postpone/put of VingTrì hoãn làm gì
4Hate VingGhet làm gì
5Admit VingThu nhan làm gì
6Avoid VingTranh làm gì
7Deny VingTừ chối làm gì
8Mind VingNgai/phien làm gì
9Miss VingLo làm gì
10Risk VingLieu làm gì
11Suggest VingGoi y làm gì
12Be busy VingBan làm gì
13Feel like VingThich/muon làm gì
14Tolerate VingChịu đựng làm gì
15Recall VingNho lai da làm gì
16Keep VingTiếp tục làm gì
17Resist VingPhan doi làm gì
18Practice VingThuc hanh làm gì
19Look forward to VingMong doi làm gì
20Imagine VingTuong tuong làm gì
21Involve VingCo liên quan làm gì
22There is no point in Ving = It is no use/good VingKhong dang làm gì
23Have difficulty in VingGap khó khăn trong viec làm gì
24Get/be accustomed to VingQuen làm gì
25Detest VingGhet làm gì

Trong cac cấu trúc:

S + spend/spent + thời gian + Ving - danh bao lau de làm gì
Ex: I spend 2 hours learning Math every day.

Sau cac giới từ: on/for...

Ex: I have to finish my homework before going out.

4. Một số trường hợp đặc biệt

Allow/permit/advise/recommend + O + to V // Ving
Ex: He allowed me to use his pencil.
He didn't allow smoking in this room.
S + have + O (chi nguoi) + V... = S + get + O (chi nguoi) + to V: bao ai làm gì do
Ex: I have my sister do housework.
I get my sister to do housework.

Cac động từ chi tri giac: hear, sound, smell, feel, watch, notice, see, listen, find,...

  • + O + Ving: thay ai đó dang làm gì.
  • + O + V: thay ai làm gì do.
Ex: I saw her get off the bus.
I smell something burning in the kitchen.

Một số trường hợp khac nhau về nghĩa giua to V va Ving:

STTCấu trúcNghia
1Remember + to V
Remember + Ving		Nho phai làm gì
Nho la da làm gì
2Forget + to V
Forget + Ving		Quen phai làm gì
Quen da làm gì
3Try + to V
Try + Ving		Co gang làm gì
Thu làm gì
4Mean + to V
Mean + Ving		Du dinh làm gì
Co nghia la
5Stop + to V
Stop + Ving		Dùng để làm gì
Dung han làm gì

exercises.question: We regret _______ you that you haven't been selected for the post of senior manager.
exercises.explanation: 'Regret + to V' = regret to say/tell (formal announcement). Here it's a formal notification.

exercises.question: Ted managed _______ my mind.
exercises.explanation: 'Manage' is always followed by 'to V': managed to change.

exercises.question: I can't afford _______ a new car.
exercises.explanation: 'Afford' is always followed by 'to V': can't afford to buy.

exercises.question: I finally finished _______ at 7:00 pm and served dinner.
exercises.explanation: 'Finish' is followed by V-ing: finished cooking.

exercises.question: The president agreed _______ the Agreement.
exercises.explanation: 'Agree' is always followed by 'to V': agreed to sign.

exercises.question: Would you mind _______ the door?
exercises.explanation: 'Would you mind' is followed by V-ing: mind closing.

exercises.question: I want _______ because I enjoy _______ people and _______ new places.
exercises.explanation: 'Want + to V'; 'enjoy + V-ing': want to travel, enjoy meeting and seeing.

exercises.question: She wanted _______ home but the boss made her _______ until she finished _______ those contracts.
exercises.explanation: 'Want + to V'; 'make + O + V(bare)'; 'finish + V-ing'.

exercises.question: We decided _______ at home.
exercises.explanation: 'Decide' is always followed by 'to V': decided to stay.

exercises.question: Frank tends _______ too much.
exercises.explanation: 'Tend' is followed by 'to V': tends to drink.

exercises.question: They spent two months _______ their house.
exercises.explanation: 'Spend + time + V-ing': spent two months renewing.

exercises.question: It takes three hours _______ to Paris.
exercises.explanation: 'It takes + time + to V': it takes three hours to get.

exercises.question: I suggested _______ a suit and tie when we went to the interview.
exercises.explanation: 'Suggest' is followed by V-ing: suggested wearing.

exercises.question: She admitted _______ the money.
exercises.explanation: 'Admit' + V-ing. 'Having stolen' = perfect gerund for an action before admitting.

exercises.question: Your shoes need _______.
exercises.explanation: 'Need + to be + PII' and 'need + V-ing' both mean the same thing. Both A and B are correct.

exercises.question: We should avoid _______ our environment.
exercises.explanation: 'Avoid' is followed by V-ing: avoid polluting.

exercises.question: I prefer _______ to _______.
exercises.explanation: 'Prefer + V-ing + to + V-ing': prefer walking to cycling.

exercises.question: The captain chose _______ with his ship.
exercises.explanation: 'Choose' is followed by 'to V': chose to die.

exercises.question: Vietnam failed _______ Tiger Cup 2004!
exercises.explanation: 'Fail' is followed by 'to V': failed to win.

exercises.question: _______ natural resources is of great importance.
exercises.explanation: Gerund (V-ing) as subject: 'Preserving natural resources is...'.

exercises.wrongSentence: There is no point in to invite him; he won't come to the party.
exercises.correctSentence: There is no point in inviting him; he won't come to the party.
exercises.explanation: 'There is no point in + V-ing', not 'to V'. After prepositions, use gerund.

exercises.wrongSentence: Do you fancy to have fish for dinner tonight?
exercises.correctSentence: Do you fancy having fish for dinner tonight?
exercises.explanation: 'Fancy' is followed by V-ing: fancy having.

exercises.wrongSentence: Some people are forced leaving their home since they can't pay for their mortgages.
exercises.correctSentence: Some people are forced to leave their home since they can't pay for their mortgages.
exercises.explanation: 'Be forced + to V': are forced to leave.

exercises.wrongSentence: I'm afraid the sweater isn't big enough for me to be wear.
exercises.correctSentence: I'm afraid the sweater isn't big enough for me to wear.
exercises.explanation: 'Enough + for sb + to V': enough for me to wear. No 'be' needed.

exercises.wrongSentence: To argue with your parents won't make thing work.
exercises.correctSentence: To argue with your parents won't make things work.
exercises.explanation: 'Things' should be plural here - 'make things work'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Adam promised to be sent us a postcard from London, but he didn't.
exercises.correctSentence: Adam promised to send us a postcard from London, but he didn't.
exercises.explanation: 'Promise + to V' (active): promised to send, not passive 'to be sent'.

exercises.wrongSentence: It takes 2 hours flying to Singapore.
exercises.correctSentence: It takes 2 hours to fly to Singapore.
exercises.explanation: 'It takes + time + to V': It takes 2 hours to fly.

exercises.wrongSentence: Would you like your parents coming home at once?
exercises.correctSentence: Would you like your parents to come home at once?
exercises.explanation: 'Would like + O + to V': would like your parents to come.

exercises.wrongSentence: Don't forget turn off the light before leaving the classroom.
exercises.correctSentence: Don't forget to turn off the light before leaving the classroom.
exercises.explanation: 'Forget + to V' = forget something you need to do.

exercises.wrongSentence: My sister practices speaks English with her friends every day.
exercises.correctSentence: My sister practices speaking English with her friends every day.
exercises.explanation: 'Practice' is followed by V-ing: practice speaking.

exercises.original: Living in a foreign country was not easy.
exercises.answer: It was not easy to live in a foreign country.
exercises.explanation: 'V-ing... + be + adj' = 'It + be + adj + to V'.

exercises.original: The coffee was very hot. I couldn't drink it.
exercises.answer: The coffee was too hot to drink.
exercises.explanation: 'Too + adj + to V' = so adj that one can't do something.

exercises.original: I haven't got much money. I cannot go on holiday this year.
exercises.answer: I haven't got enough money to go on holiday this year.
exercises.explanation: 'Enough + N + to V' = sufficient amount to do something.

exercises.original: My father prefer listening to the radio to watching TV.
exercises.answer: My father would rather listen to the radio than watch TV.
exercises.explanation: 'Would rather + V + than + V' = prefer one thing to another.

exercises.original: She washed her hair before she went out.
exercises.answer: She washed her hair before going out.
exercises.explanation: After prepositions like 'before', use V-ing: before going out.

exercises.original: Henry had better not buy too many trainers. He spent a lot of money.
exercises.answer: Henry spent a lot of money buying too many trainers.
exercises.explanation: 'Spend + money/time + V-ing': spent a lot of money buying.

exercises.original: Teaching children at this age is extremely difficult.
exercises.answer: It is extremely difficult to teach children at this age.
exercises.explanation: 'V-ing + be + adj' = 'It + be + adj + to V'.

exercises.original: My brother is very strong. He can lift a horse.
exercises.answer: My brother is strong enough to lift a horse.
exercises.explanation: 'Adj + enough + to V' = sufficiently adj to do something.

exercises.original: That car is quite expensive. I cannot buy it.
exercises.answer: That car is too expensive for me to buy.
exercises.explanation: 'Too + adj + for sb + to V'.

exercises.original: My son prefer playing chess than flying kites.
exercises.answer: My son would rather play chess than fly kites.
exercises.explanation: 'Would rather + V + than + V'.
